name: Credit.Org - North Carolina Branch
description_short: Nonprofit credit counseling and HUD-approved housing advisor serving since 1974. Offers free debt relief, credit building, and homeownership guidance through certified counselors.
description_long:
Credit.org is a national nonprofit organization that has been providing credit and housing counseling services since 1974. The organization operates as a HUD-approved housing counselor network with NFCC certification, delivering financial guidance to millions of consumers nationwide. The North Carolina branch is part of this larger national infrastructure dedicated to financial education and debt relief assistance.

The organization offers comprehensive free counseling services across multiple financial domains: debt relief and management, credit building and repair, housing preparation (pre-purchase counseling, reverse mortgage education, foreclosure prevention, disaster recovery), rental readiness assistance, and bankruptcy guidance. They provide personalized one-on-one counseling sessions via phone and in-person appointments, complemented by self-paced online courses (FIT Academy), live webinars, and financial calculators. All counseling sessions are free and require no pressure sales tactics, with certified nonprofit counselors guiding consumers through personalized financial plans.

Credit.org distinguishes itself through its scale and longevity: the organization reports delivering $2.2 billion in homeowner support, completing over 5.2 million counseling sessions, and reaching 87,000+ online education participants. They offer specialized reverse mortgage counseling (both HECM and non-HECM), which is a regulated requirement in the reverse mortgage industry. The organization maintains partnerships with national and community organizations and provides financial wellness events for employers. Their bilingual services (English/Spanish) and multiple appointment scheduling options increase accessibility.

As a legitimate nonprofit counselor, Credit.org is best suited for consumers seeking genuine financial education and debt management strategies without sales pressure. The primary caveat is that this is educational counseling rather than debt settlement or legal services—they guide you toward solutions but don't negotiate with creditors or file bankruptcy on your behalf. Consumers needing urgent debt settlement or legal bankruptcy representation would require separate providers.
pros: Completely free counseling sessions with no hidden fees or sales pressure
NFCC-certified and HUD-approved counselors with documented expertise since 1974
Comprehensive service range covering debt, credit, housing, rental, and reverse mortgages
Personalized financial plans created during initial consultation
Free self-paced FIT Academy courses and live interactive webinars
Bilingual services in English and Spanish
Specialized reverse mortgage counseling with required HECM and non-HECM options
Track record of 5.2+ million counseling sessions and $2.2 billion in homeowner support
Multiple access methods: phone (800-431-8157/800-431-8695), online scheduling, in-person appointments
cons: Counseling is educational guidance only—they do not negotiate directly with creditors or settle debts on your behalf
Cannot file bankruptcy or provide legal representation; bankruptcy guidance is informational only
Requires scheduling appointments; not instant or on-demand service for immediate crisis situations
As a nonprofit, capacity may vary by location; wait times not disclosed on website
Website lacks transparent information about specific success rates or outcome metrics beyond total sessions completed
best_for: Consumers seeking free, unbiased financial education and debt management planning without sales pressure
First-time homebuyers exploring down payment options and pre-purchase mortgage education
Seniors evaluating reverse mortgage options before committing to the loan process
Individuals facing foreclosure or mortgage default seeking assistance and prevention strategies
Renters working to improve credit and rental readiness in competitive housing markets
diagnosis: Credit.org is best for consumers seeking legitimate free financial counseling and education from certified nonprofit advisors without sales pressure or fees. The critical caveat is that they provide guidance and planning, not active debt settlement, creditor negotiation, or legal services—making them ideal for financial education but not for immediate debt crisis intervention or bankruptcy filing.
services: Free one-on-one debt relief and financial counseling sessions
Credit building and credit score improvement counseling
Credit report review and dispute guidance
Debt management plan creation and financial planning
Pre-purchase homebuying education and mortgage readiness counseling
Reverse mortgage counseling (HECM and non-HECM required counseling)
Foreclosure prevention and mortgage default assistance
Rental readiness counseling and rental help for unaffordable rent situations
FIT Academy self-paced online courses (debt, credit, budgeting, homeownership, foreclosure prevention)
Live webinars and interactive financial wellness classes
Financial calculators for budgeting, debt management, and retirement planning
typical_results_timeline: Counseling available within 1-2 weeks of contact.
address: 615 Saint George Square Ct, Suite 300, Winston Salem, NC 27103
